figures.caption: Figure 1: Structure of ST-PT: a 2D factor graph on (channel i, time-patch t) coordinates with Z/H/G latent variables, unary/ternary/binary factors, and a joint softmax over temporal and cross-channel dependencies.
Table 2: Few-shot synthetic forecasting (Test MSE, lower is better). Prior-injected ST-PT variants are strongest at N = 150, with the advantage shrinking as N grows.

Table 9: Full cornerstone performace validation: Searched hyperparameter results (Test MSE / MAE) on seven datasets at four forecasting horizons(fh), under hyperparameter search. The ST-PT cornerstone is compared with five strong 2023-era baselines. TSMixer numbers are from the TSMixer paper [29]; TimeMixer, PatchTST, TimesNet, and Crossformer numbers are from the TimeMixer paper [30]

Figure 2: (a) Three graph-level mechanisms for injecting symbolic priors into ST-PT’s factor graph (RQ1, Section 4.1): periodicity multiplies the temporal message-F logits by a per-channel cosine similarity matrix (potential modulation); trend augments the graph with a per-channel latent HMM chain coupled to Z (node augmentation); lag adds new bilinear pairwise edges between causally linked channels at a known temporal offset (factor engineering). (b) Computation flow of the RQ3 latent-space AR model (Section 4.3, Appendix G): a causal encoder MFVI turns the history patches into a latent cache, and each AR step infers the next-slice latent by a single-step MFVI update that fuses a dual-pathway unary (re-encoded prediction + transition prior), temporal messages from the cache, cross-channel messages among the simultaneous new latents, and a global topic.

Table 1: Cornerstone validation: long-term forecasting on seven benchmarks, Test MSE / MAE averaged over four horizons {96, 192, 336, 720} (lower is better). The bare ST-PT cornerstone is broadly comparable to strong 2023 baselines on every benchmark, without any RQ-specific lever activated. Per-horizon raw numbers and the experimental setting are in Appendix E.

Table 8: Long-horizon autoregressive forecasting on six benchmarks at pred_len ∈ {96, 192}. Baseline columns are Test MSE only (MAE not available in the source logs); our MFVI-based latent AR model reports both Test MSE and Test MAE. The MFVI-based latent AR model leads on every dataset–horizon combination in MSE.

Table 5: Real-data prior study with varying training-sample fractions. p5 / p100 denote runs that use 5% / 100% of the standard training split, respectively. Metrics are Test MSE / MAE. The Trend prior helps on ETTh1, ETTh2 and Weather at p5—datasets with trend-dominated low-frequency structure—and becomes neutral or slightly harmful at p100, consistent with the synthetic scaling law that prior advantage shrinks as N grows.

Table 6: Conditional time series generation on ConTSG-Bench: per-(dataset, metric) average rank across the 10 datasets (lower is better; 1 = best, 11 = worst). Last two columns report the overall average rank and the number of top-1 finishes out of 10×6 = 60 (dataset, metric) pairs. Raw numbers (mean±std over three seeds for baselines and for PT-FG) are in Appendix F.
